name: National Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail sales.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn store operator with over 3,300 retail locations spanning 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is listed on both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating substantial scale and institutional credibility in the alternative finance space.

FirstCash's core business model focuses on serving cash and credit-constrained consumers through multiple revenue streams: non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property, retail sales of used merchandise, gold and precious metal purchasing, and layaway services. They buy and sell a diverse range of items including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Additionally, through their subsidiary AFF, FirstCash provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions across a network of over 15,000 active retail merchant partner locations.

The company distinguishes itself through its extensive geographic footprint and dual-business model combining traditional pawn operations with technology-driven payment solutions via AFF. Their scale of 3,300+ locations provides accessibility and consistent branding across multiple markets. The non-recourse nature of pawn loans (where borrowers are not personally liable beyond forfeiting collateral) offers a structured alternative to payday loans without ongoing collection risk for consumers.

As a pawn lender, FirstCash serves consumers facing immediate cash needs but carries inherent limitations: pawn loans require valuable collateral, involve asset liquidation, and may result in loss of personal items of sentimental value. While the company provides legitimate alternative lending, consumers should understand that pawn loans are fundamentally short-term solutions secured by tangible property rather than credit-building instruments.
address: 6223 E Truman Rd, Kansas City, MO 64126

company_info.headquarters: 6223 E Truman Rd, Kansas City, MO 64126

pros: 3,300+ retail locations across 29 U.S. states plus UK and Latin America provide extensive accessibility
Non-recourse pawn loans mean borrowers are not personally liable beyond forfeiting collateral
Fast cash on the spot for items of value with no credit check required
Diverse merchandise inventory (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, instruments) appeals to multiple consumer needs
Layaway service available with 10% down payment for budget-conscious shoppers
Gold and precious metal buying services provide immediate liquidity for precious items
Established publicly-traded company (S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000) with institutional oversight
cons: Pawn loans require valuable collateral—those without pledgeable assets cannot borrow
Risk of permanent loss of personal property if loan is not repaid within agreed terms
Short-term lending solution that does not build credit history or address underlying cash flow problems
Interest rates and terms not disclosed on website; actual cost of borrowing varies by location and item value
Pawn model inherently advantages the lender; consumers often recover significantly less than retail value of items
best_for: Consumers with valuable personal property (jewelry, electronics, instruments) needing immediate short-term cash
Individuals without access to traditional credit who need collateral-based lending
Shoppers seeking to purchase used merchandise or layaway items at pawn store retail locations
People with gold or precious metals they wish to sell quickly for cash
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers facing immediate cash needs who own valuable personal property they can temporarily pledge as collateral, particularly those without access to traditional credit. The main caveat is that pawn loans are short-term, collateral-dependent solutions that do not build credit and carry risk of permanent asset loss—they address immediate liquidity problems but not underlying financial instability.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property pledges
Retail sales of used jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments
Gold and precious metal buying services
Layaway services with 10% down payment option
Item appraisal and valuation for pawn collateral
Lease-to-own financing through subsidiary AFF at 15,000+ retail merchant partners
Retail point-of-sale payment solutions via AFF subsidiary
Store locator and inventory search tools
Merchandise buying services (company purchases items of value)
Online and in-store shopping for used goods
Consumer lending focused on cash and credit-constrained individuals
